description: Diagnostic decision tree for A2681 (MacBook Air 13" M2 PJM-MLB). Follow sequentially from Step 1. Has MagSafe (J5401). Two USB-C ports left side (ATC0/ATC1). SoC: M2 T8112.
WARNING: A2681 (820-02536) M2 Air ONLY. SoC is M2 T8112 (NOT M1 T8103). Comparator is U9810 (NOT U8110 as on M1 boards). Has MagSafe J5401 (pages 27-29) — M1 Air A2337 does NOT have MagSafe. SVR AON is 30A ICC MAX, 3-phase, U5700 controller (same as M1). PBUS E-Fuse on page 78 is M2-specific — M1 boards do not have this. SPMU is U8100 (pages 33-36). Bootstrap path PP5V_S2 to PP3V8_AON VDRV via R5900 on page 32.
data_source: Schematic PNGs (pages 14-22, 25-29, 32-48, 63-64, 69, 71-76, 78-80, 82-87) cross-referenced with board-level repair knowledge. Power-on sequence verified against Eason reference (iphone_cpu_fix) M1/M2 standby circuit PDF.

eason_reference.source: M1 series standby circuit and power-on principle, and 20V boost circuit (Eason @ iphone_cpu_fix)
power_on_sequence: 1. PPVBUS_USBC0 5V → CD3217 MOSFET → PPHV_INT0_AONSW → fuse → PPDCIN_AONSW (or MagSafe J5401 path, pages 27-29)
2. PPDCIN_AONSW → R5220 → PPDCIN_AON_CHGR_R → U5200 charger IC (page 25). R5215/R5216 → CHGR_AUX_DET → U5200 detect
3. U5200 → Q5230/Q5240 → PPBUS_AON (12.6V temp). CHGR_EN_MVR → U5340 → P3V8AON_PWR_EN → R5710 → U5700
5. PP3V8_AON_VDDMAIN → U8100(SPMU) + U9810 comparator (page 32). R-divider → MPMU_SNS_DIV → U9810. When same>opposite, PMU_VDD_HI(3.8V) → MPMU voltage detect
6. MPMU → all MPMU rails (pages 37-43) + PP3V3_S2_UPC/PP1V8_S2 to CD3217. UC-series → PP3V3_AON, PP1V8_AON. U8100(SPMU) → SPMU rails (pages 33-36)
7. M2 T8112 hard start (24MHz crystal + Y-series 32.768KHz + PMU_RESET_L). M2 reads ROM, I2C to CD3217
8. M2 → MPMU → P5VS2_PWR_EN → PP5V_S2 (page 45). Bootstrap via R5900 (page 32) PP5V_S2 to PP3V8_AON VDRV
9. CD3217 reads shared ROM (4 SPI lines), sends PD via CC1/CC2 → charger boosts to 20V
10. 20V → CD3217 MOSFET → PPDCIN_AONSW → U5200 → real PPBUS_AON (12.6V regulated)

forced_start_bypass.description: Bypass protective isolation to test if fault is pre-SVR or post-SVR. Uses U9810 comparator (M2 equivalent of U8110 on M1). Critical diagnostic shortcut from Eason reference.
steps: 1. Inject 3.8V on PP3V8_AON_VDDMAIN externally (use 4.3V if testing with display attached)
2. Jumper U9810 output to force PMU_VDD_HI high (NOT U8110 — that is M1, U9810 is on page 32 for A2681)
3. Press start button OR connect charger for 3 seconds

interpretation.forced_start_works: Fault is in the FRONT END — protection isolation and 5V→20V boost circuit (CD3217, charger IC, fuses, MagSafe path). Check Steps 1-2 of diagnostic tree.
interpretation.forced_start_fails: Fault is in the BACK END — M2 working conditions (PMU, SoC power, clocks, ROM). Check Steps 3+ of diagnostic tree.

ppvaon_fault_l.description: Single-pin SVR fault check. Active-low fault output from U5700. If low, U5700 reports internal fault — check phase FETs, inductor shorts, overcurrent. If high, SVR AON considers itself healthy.

pmu_active_ready.description: PMU finished startup? If high, MPMU has completed its boot sequence and all enabled bucks are running. If low or absent, MPMU is stuck — check PMU_VDD_HI, 32.768kHz clock, PMU_RESET_L.

crystal_32khz.description: 32.768kHz heartbeat alive? Probe the crystal oscillator for the MPMU. If no oscillation, MPMU cannot complete startup. Check for cracked crystal, cold solder, or contamination on pads.

soc_force_dfu.description: Hardware vs software differentiation. If SOC_FORCE_DFU is asserted and board enters DFU, the SoC hardware is alive but firmware/NAND is corrupt. If DFU fails, SoC or PMU hardware fault. Check page 44 for PMU support aliases.

phase_balance.description: Measure voltage drop across RS710, RS711, RS712 to verify all three SVR AON phases are carrying equal current. Imbalanced phases indicate a dead phase driver FET (Q5800/Q5820/Q5840) or open inductor (L5800/L5820/L5840). Each sense resistor feeds back to U5700 for current balancing.

cd3217_sys_detect.description: CD3217 needs SYS_DETECT from battery to confirm system presence before enabling PD negotiation. Check battery connector and SYS_DETECT line. If SYS_DETECT is missing, CD3217 will not negotiate 20V — board stays at 5V. Check CD3217 on page 25, charger support on page 26.

pbus_efuse.description: M2-specific PBUS E-Fuse (page 78). Not present on M1 boards. Check for blown e-fuse which would cut PPBUS distribution to downstream loads. Verify continuity across e-fuse. If open, downstream short blew it — find the short before replacing.

pp5v_s2_bootstrap.description: PP5V_S2 to PP3V8_AON VDRV bootstrap path via R5900 on page 32. This feeds back from the S2 domain to sustain the AON SVR driver voltage after initial startup. If R5900 is open or damaged, SVR AON may lose VDRV after S2 comes up, causing a boot loop or intermittent shutdown.

tree.probe: Bulk capacitors near U5200 charger IC output (page 25). Also check MagSafe path J5401 (pages 27-29) as A2681 has MagSafe.

no.diagnosis: Charger path fault — check both USB-C and MagSafe input paths
check_sequence: Check PPVBUS_USBC0/USBC1 at USB-C connector pins (pages 63-64) — should have 5V or 20V from adapter
Check MagSafe J5401 connector (pages 27-29) — A2681 has MagSafe, verify MagSafe PPDCIN path and detection circuit
Check CD3217 port controllers on pages 63-64 — CD3217 failure is a common cause of no-power on M2 Air
Check SPI ROM for CD3217 — if corrupted, CD3217 will not negotiate PD
Check SYS_DETECT from battery connector — charger needs battery detect signal
Check charger IC U5200 (page 25) gate drives and MOSFET switches
Check charger support circuitry (page 26)
Check PBUS E-Fuse (page 78) — M2-specific, if blown no PPBUS distribution
Thermal cam on charger area for shorted FETs

tree.probe: PP3V8_AON output caps near U5700 SVR AON controller (page 32). Check VDDMAIN branch.

check_sequence: Check PPVAON_PWR_EN enable — derived from charger IC via delay circuit
Check PPVAON_FAULT_L (page 32) — SHORTCUT: if low, U5700 reports fault condition
Check phase balance via RS710/RS711/RS712 sense resistors (page 32) — imbalance indicates dead phase
Diode mode on PP3V8_AON output caps — check for short on VDDMAIN branch
Check PP3V8_AON_VDDMAIN separately from other AON branches
Check 3-phase driver FETs: Q5800 (DRVL1 via L5800), Q5820 (DRVL2 via L5820), Q5840 (DRVL3 via L5840)
Check R5900 bootstrap resistor (page 32) — PP5V_S2 to PP3V8_AON VDRV path, if open SVR may not sustain
Thermal cam on U5700 and phase FETs Q5800/Q5820/Q5840 at low voltage (5V current-limited)
SHORTCUT: Try Eason forced start bypass — inject 3.8V on PP3V8_AON_VDDMAIN + jumper U9810 output + press start. If board boots, fault is upstream of SVR.

tree.probe: UC-series LDO outputs. PP3V3_AON and PP1V8_AON output capacitors near SPMU area (pages 33-36).

check_sequence: Check PP3V8_AON input to LDOs — if missing, fault is upstream at U5700 SVR AON
If PP3V3_AON missing: check LDO IC input/output, diode mode on output cap
If PP1V8_AON missing: check LDO IC input/output, diode mode on output cap
Thermal cam on LDO area for hot component indicating downstream short

tree.probe: SPMU U8100 buck output caps (pages 33-36). Multiple buck regulators with large cap banks.
tree.expect: Multiple SPMU buck rails present. Check individual buck outputs for expected voltages.

check_sequence: Check PP3V8_AON_VDDMAIN input to U8100 (pages 33-36)
Check SPMU buck enable signals on pages 33-36
Check PP5V_MPMU_BSTLQ boost converter output — SPMU needs this rail
Diode mode across individual SPMU buck output cap banks
Check for cap short on individual buck outputs — isolate which buck is missing
Thermal cam on U8100 area — if SPMU is hot, internal short or downstream loading
If single buck missing: find downstream short on that rail before replacing SPMU

tree.probe: MPMU buck output caps (pages 37-43). Check PMU support page 44 for PMU_ACTIVE_READY, SOC_FORCE_DFU, 32.768kHz clock.
tree.expect: Multiple MPMU buck rails present — PCPU, ECPU, GPU, SOC domain voltages. PMU_ACTIVE_READY should go high.

no.diagnosis: MPMU fault — check comparator and clock first
check_sequence: Check PP3V8_AON_VDDMAIN input to MPMU (pages 37-43)

CRITICAL (Eason ref): Check U9810 comparator (page 32) — R-divider feeds MPMU_SNS_DIV to U9810 same-direction input. PP3V8_AON feeds opposite input. When same > opposite, U9810 outputs PMU_VDD_HI (3.8V) for MPMU voltage detect. Without PMU_VDD_HI, MPMU will NOT start. NOTE: U9810 is the M2 equivalent of U8110 on M1.
Check 32.768kHz crystal — MPMU needs this clock to start (per Eason reference). Probe for oscillation.
Check PMU_ONOFF_L — pulled up by PP1V8_AON_MPMU
Check PMU_ACTIVE_READY output (page 44) — SHORTCUT: if not going high, MPMU is stuck
Check SOC_FORCE_DFU (page 44) — if asserting, SoC hardware may be alive but firmware bad
Check PMU_RESET_L — must be released for MPMU to start
Check individual MPMU buck enable signals (pages 37-43)
Diode mode across MPMU buck output cap banks
Thermal cam on MPMU area

tree.probe: PP5V_S2 output (page 45). PP3V3_S2 output (page 47). Load switches on page 48.

no.diagnosis: Standalone VR enable or IC fault — S2 domain rails
check_sequence: Check P5VS2_PWR_EN enable signal (page 45) — gated by PMU GPIO, only asserted AFTER M2 hard start
Check PP5V_S2 VR IC input (PPBUS_AON), output inductor, sense resistor on page 45
Check R5900 bootstrap (page 32) — if R5900 is open, PP5V_S2 may come up but SVR AON VDRV drops out causing boot loop
Check PP3V3_S2 VR and P3V3S2_PWR_EN gating (page 47)
Check load switches on page 48 — downstream loads may be pulling S2 rails down
Diode mode on PP5V_S2 and PP3V3_S2 output caps for downstream shorts

tree.probe: SoC power pages 14-22. PCPU, ECPU, GPU, DDR, SOC domains from MPMU/SPMU bucks feeding M2 T8112.
tree.expect: Multiple voltage domains present at SoC pins. Board should attempt boot.

no.diagnosis: SoC fault or SPI ROM fault
check_sequence: Check SPI NOR flash — if M2 cannot read firmware, no boot
Check secure element
Check SoC ground connections on SoC power pages — open ground can cause no-boot
Check NAND/storage power (pages 71-73) — M2 needs storage for boot
Check SOC_FORCE_DFU (page 44) — if DFU works, SoC is alive but firmware/NAND is bad
If all power present but no Apple logo: try DFU mode via USB-C

substeps.probe: eDP interface (page 75), display sequencer (page 76). Check backlight boost and display power rails.
substeps.signals: eDP data lanes, backlight PWM, display power enable sequence
substeps.note: A2681 display uses eDP (page 75) with dedicated sequencer (page 76). Different topology from M1 A2337.

substeps.probe: J5401 MagSafe connector (pages 27-29). Check MagSafe detection, PPDCIN path, LED feedback.
substeps.note: A2681 HAS MagSafe — this is a key difference from M1 Air A2337. MagSafe provides alternative charging path alongside USB-C. Check connector alignment, pin corrosion, detection circuit.

substeps.probe: ATC0 and ATC1 port controllers (pages 63-64). Both ports on left side of chassis.
substeps.note: Two USB-C ports left side. CD3217 port controllers handle PD negotiation, data mux, and SPI ROM access. CD3217 failure remains a common IC fault on M2 boards.

substeps.probe: Keyboard controller and matrix (page 85). Check keyboard backlight and membrane connections.
substeps.note: Keyboard is historically the highest single fault category on Apple Silicon MacBook Airs. Check for liquid damage on keyboard flex and connector.

substeps.probe: Audio codec and amplifiers (pages 82-84). San Diego PMU audio support (pages 79-80).
substeps.signals: I2S data, amplifier enables, speaker connector signals
substeps.note: San Diego PMU (pages 79-80) provides audio-related power. Check both codec and amplifier stages. Audio IC failures documented on M-series boards.

substeps.probe: WiFi/BT module (page 69). Check antenna connections and module power rails.
substeps.note: WiFi/BT module is soldered — rework requires BGA skills. Check for cold solder or pad corrosion.

substeps.probe: SSD0 (pages 71-73). Check NAND power rails, SSD controller, and data lines.
substeps.note: M2 NAND is paired to SoC. If NAND fault confirmed, board may be unrepairable without SoC transplant. Check pages 71-73 for full SSD0 topology.

substeps.probe: Trackpad controller and connector (pages 86-87). Check SPI interface and LDO power.
substeps.note: Check trackpad flex for liquid damage. Trackpad issues often co-occur with keyboard faults from liquid spills.
